body.summary: LOCK 009 review complete. Approved with 3 safety recommendations.

review_items.description: trace_id = trace-[task_id] is deterministic but fragile if task_id contains special characters or is empty. Empty task_id creates trace_id of just 'trace-' which collides.
review_items.suggestion: Add guard: if task_id is empty/null, use 'trace-unknown-{hash_of_input}'. Sanitize special characters.

review_items.description: Spec says 'Must not crash on null, {}, {task_id}' but doesn't define behavior for malformed nested input like {classification: 'string instead of object'}. Node.js JSON parsing won't crash, but downstream type coercion could.
review_items.suggestion: Add schema validation wrapper. If classification isn't {reasons, warnings} format, wrap it as {reasons: [], warnings: ['malformed classification input']}.

review_items.description: Only file write allowed is Markdown report. Spec doesn't define WHERE the report is written. If code defaults to CWD and npm scripts run from Tripp.Control root, it could clobber existing files.
review_items.suggestion: Lock report output to D:\Echos.House\builds\apps\Tripp.Control\reports\ only. Create directory if missing. Never write outside that path.

review_items.description: 14 test cases is solid. From local perspective, add test case for Windows path handling (backslashes in input task_id, Windows line endings in report output).
review_items.suggestion: Add Test 15: Windows path safety (task_id: 'modules\auth', verify trace_id sanitized).
